verdict_reasoning: KLK2 was traditionally characterized as a secreted serine protease, with dominant cytoplasmic/ER-translocated/extracellular compartmentalization. However, robust evidence now demonstrates cell surface expression in prostate cancer cells and dissociated metastatic tissue (FACS-confirmed, confocal microscopy with EpCAM colocalization). Multiple surface-directed therapeutics (CD3-KLK2 bispecific, radioligand therapy, antibody drug conjugates) show clinical/preclinical efficacy, validating stable surface accessibility. The protein lacks an intrinsic TM domain or GPI anchor, reaching the surface via canonical secretory pathway in a context-dependent (cancer cell state) manner. Surface pool is likely sustained but mechanism is unknown—possibly non-covalent binding to TM partner or altered trafficking in neoplastic state. Qualifies as contextual: documented surface expression in specific cells/disease context, therapeutically accessible, but not via constitutive intrinsic anchoring mechanism.
